title: Textron (TXT) Launches Business Jet Cessna Citation Ascend
text: Textron's (TXT) arm, Textron Aviation, unveils the newest addition to its business jet portfolio, Cessna Citation Ascend.

title: Textron Aviation celebrates its 100th flagship Cessna Citation Longitude production unit
text: GENEVA, Switzerland--(BUSINESS WIRE)--Textron Aviation today announced that the 100th production unit of the company's flagship Cessna Citation Longitude business jet rolled out of the factory and is expected to deliver later this year. The Cessna Citation Longitude super-midsize business jet is designed, produced and delivered by Textron Aviation Inc., a Textron Inc. (NYSE:TXT) company. Textron Aviation employees celebrated this significant milestone with a special celebration at Textron Aviat.

title: Textron Aviation delivers first passenger unit of Cessna SkyCourier large-utility turboprop
text: WICHITA, Kan.--(BUSINESS WIRE)--Textron Aviation today announced the recent delivery of the first passenger unit of the twin-engine, large-utility turboprop, the Cessna SkyCourier, for use by Western Aircraft, Inc., doing business in Hawaii as Lāna'i Air. The company offers luxury air service connecting Oahu to the island of Lāna'i. The Cessna SkyCourier is designed and produced by Textron Aviation Inc., a Textron Inc. (NYSE:TXT) company. “The Cessna SkyCourier is well on its way to becoming a.

title: Textron Aviation Unveils Newest Addition to Bestselling Business Jet Family: Cessna Citation Ascend
text: GENEVA--(BUSINESS WIRE)--Textron Aviation announced today the newest Cessna Citation business jet in the legendary 560XL series — the Cessna Citation Ascend. Unveiled on the eve of this week's European Business Aviation Convention and Exhibition (EBACE), the Citation Ascend is designed to bring an entirely new cockpit, improved performance and a more luxurious cabin to the midsize business jet market. Attendees will enjoy access to a new Citation Ascend mock up debuting during the show. Current.

title: Textron Aviation Brings Simulator Options to Customers in Europe
text: WICHITA, Kan.--(BUSINESS WIRE)--Textron Aviation announced today expanded support for business jet customers in Europe with the addition of a new Cessna Citation CJ3+/Cessna Citation M2 Gen2 Convertible Full-Flight Simulator in the region. The new simulator is scheduled to be ready for training at FlightSafety International's Farnborough, England Learning Center in January 2025. The Cessna Citation business jet family is designed and manufactured by Textron Aviation Inc., a Textron Inc. (NYSE:T.

title: Intuit QuickBooks Launches QuickBooks Online Accountant in More Than 170 Countries Around the World, Including Panama
text: PANAMA CITY--(BUSINESS WIRE)--Intuit QuickBooks today announces global free access to QuickBooks Online Accountant (QBOA), enabling accounting professionals in 170 countries to move to the cloud faster, scale their practices and bring more value to their clients and the wider economy. Accounting professionals make a huge contribution to the economy at a local, regional and global level, working as trusted business partners to the SMB community, which represents 90% of businesses and accounts for 70% of employment globally1. But their impact could be even bigger, as QuickBooks research suggests accountants are now busier than ever. Four out of 10 (43%) report they have more than 40 clients – and 86% are looking to technology to help them be more efficient and better meet client needs. QBOA addresses this by enabling accountants to manage their practice workflow, client interactions and billing all in one place. Developed with a select group of accountants globally over the past 12 months, QBOA is a refined suite of benefits to help professional accountants move to the cloud faster and drive greater efficiency and scalability of their firms. Access to real time insights from anywhere in the world puts professional accountants at the heart of driving informed business decisions that could positively increase small business survival rates – currently, only 51.6% of U.S. small businesses survive beyond the first five years2. Intuit serves more than 100 million customers worldwide and is one of the biggest software companies in the world, with Intuit QuickBooks now the first provider in the market to deliver at this scale for the accounting profession. Lars Leber, Vice President of Intuit QuickBooks Australia, said: “The scale of this roll out is unlike anything that has been done before. Providing QBOA as a free resource to anyone around the world will positively support accountants and their small business clients across the globe. The opportunity for impact in Panama is particularly exciting; the country is rated highly in The Ease of Doing Business Report in terms of setting up a business and trade opportunities, but financial management is often a pain point. The global, free rollout of QBOA will arm accountants in the region with the most efficient financial management solutions, proving the difference for a business-ready community.” Global trends and technological advancements are transforming the world of work and reshaping the future of the accountancy profession, increasingly shifting demand towards more strategic and analytical roles3. Accountants will be key to helping fuel local business and economic prosperity. QuickBooks Online Accountant benefits professional accountants anywhere in the world – through the following set of features: Singular login and dashboard view of real-time and historic client data, current projects and deadlines; Automated transactions and specific rules classifying data, minimizing manual data entries and reducing risk of errors; Options to use workflows to send reminders to the right people at the right time and automate common tasks –invaluable for bookkeepers in busiest periods of the year; Integrated apps providing functionality that spans reporting, inventory management, expense management, payroll, eCommerce; Predictive AI-based ability to estimate income tax/VAT contributions, support self-assessment filings with automated breakdown of earnings and tax-deductible expense. Assisted QuickBooks Online Accountant onboarding for accountant professionals and bookkeepers is free, via a Zoom session, and is focused on new clients who are new to QuickBooks, as well as any existing clients that aren’t actively using QuickBooks. It’s a hands-on, “Do-It-With-Me” session. To celebrate the launch of the QBOA global edition, limited time only pricing bundles have also been announced. To help accountants get their small business clients started on QuickBooks online, for just the next three months, the one-dollar subscription deal will be active. One dollar (or country currency equivalent), per month, per file. The deal is available in all countries, to further support digital expansion for all accountants. Local developers can now publish apps as part of the QuickBooks Global App Store – to include payroll, payments, inventory and purpose-built applications for specific regions – to integrate with QuickBooks. For more information and information on how to access the free QuickBooks Online Accountant platform please visit here. - ENDS - About Intuit Intuit is the global financial technology platform that powers prosperity for the people and communities we serve. With more than 100 million customers worldwide using TurboTax, Credit Karma, QuickBooks, and Mailchimp, we believe that everyone should have the opportunity to prosper. We never stop working to find new, innovative ways to make that possible. Please visit us for the latest information about Intuit, our products and services, and find us on social. 1 World Economic Forum White Paper “Future Readiness of SMEs: Mobilizing the SME Sector to Drive Widespread Sustainability and Prosperity” published 30 November 2021: https://www.weforum.org/whitepapers/future-readiness-of-smes-mobilizing-the-sme-sector-to-drive-widespread-sustainability-and-prosperity 2 The US Bureau of Labor Statistics, 1994-2022: https://www.bls.gov/bdm/us_age_naics_00_table7.txt 3 ACCA (the Association of Chartered Certified Accountants) “Future ready: accountancy careers in the 2020s” report. Link: https://www.accaglobal.com/content/dam/ACCA_Global/professional-insights/FutureReady2020s/JamieLyon.FutureCareersAccoutancy2020s.summary.pdf

title: Intuit QuickBooks anuncia el lanzamiento de QuickBooks Online Accountant en más de 170 países, incluso en Panamá
text: CIUDAD DE PANAMÁ--(BUSINESS WIRE)--Intuit QuickBooks anuncia hoy el acceso gratuito a QuickBooks Online Accountant (QBOA), que les permite a los contadores de 170 países trabajar en la nube de manera más rápida, mejorar sus servicios y ofrecerle un valor agregado no solo a sus clientes sino también a la economía en general. Los contadores profesionales hacen un gran aporte a la economía local, regional y mundial, ya que son socios de confianza de todas las PYME, que representan al 90% de las empresas en total y que generan el 70% del empleo1. Sin embargo, su impacto podría ser aún mayor, tal como sugiere la investigación de QuickBooks que sugiere que los contadores tienen más trabajo que nunca. Cuatro de cada diez contadores (un 43%) informó tener más de 40 clientes. Otro 86% está en la búsqueda de tecnologías que los ayuden a ser más eficientes y a poder resolver las necesidades de sus clientes de una mejor manera. QBOA aborda esta cuestión porque permite que los contadores puedan manejar su flujo de trabajo, las interacciones con los clientes y la facturación, todo en un solo lugar. Gracias al desarrollo de un grupo selecto de contadores de todo el mundo que trabajaron durante los últimos 12 meses, QBOA logró convertirse en un paquete de trabajo mejorado con beneficios para que los contadores puedan pasar a la nube de manera rápida y tener una mayor eficiencia y escalabilidad en sus empresas. El poder acceder a la información en tiempo real desde cualquier lugar del mundo pone a los contadores en el centro de la toma de decisiones empresariales, las cuales podrían aumentar de manera positiva los índices de supervivencia de las pequeñas empresas que, actualmente, indican que solo un 51,6% de las pequeñas empresas de Estados Unidos se pueden sostener más allá de los cinco primeros años2. Intuit ayuda a más de 100 millones de clientes de todo el mundo y es una de las empresas de software más grandes que existen. Ahora, se le suma Intuit Quickbook, el primer proveedor del mercado que ofrece este tipo de servicios a esta escala para la profesión contable. Lars Leber, vicepresidente de Intuit QuickBooks Australia, declaró: “La escala del lanzamiento no tiene precedentes. Permitir el acceso a QBOA de manera gratuita y en todas partes del mundo será una ayuda valiosa para los contadores y su clientela de pequeñas empresas. La oportunidad de impactar en Panamá trae un entusiasmo particular; el país está muy bien calificado según el índice de facilidad para hacer negocios (Ease of Doing Business) en cuanto a la creación de una empresa y a las posibilidades de comercio. Sin embargo, la gestión financiera suele ser un punto débil en el país. Gracias al uso mundial y gratuito de QBOA, los contadores de las distintas regiones tendrán soluciones de gestión financiera eficaces, y hará la diferencia en las comunidades que estén preparadas para hacer negocios". Las tendencias mundiales y los avances tecnológicos están cambiando nuestra manera de trabajar y están remodelando el futuro del trabajo contable, que hace que haya una mayor demanda de roles que sean más estratégicos y analíticos.3. Los contadores serán un eje central en el crecimiento de las empresas locales y en la prosperidad de la economía. QuickBooks Online Accountant es un beneficio para todos los contadores gracias a que proporciona las siguientes funciones: Inicio de sesión único, y visualización del panel de control con los datos de los clientes (ya sean históricos o en tiempo real), los proyectos actuales y las fechas de entrega; Transacciones automatizadas y reglas específicas para clasificar datos, que minimizan las entradas manuales de datos y reducen el riesgo de cometer errores; Posibilidad de usar flujos de trabajo para enviar recordatorios a destinatarios específicos en un momento determinado y automatizar tareas básicas, muy valioso para los momentos del año en los que hay mucho trabajo; Aplicaciones integradas con funciones para hacer informes, gestionar inventarios, gastos, nóminas y comercios virtuales (eCommerce); Capacidad predictiva basada en IA para estimar las contribuciones al impuesto sobre la renta/IVA, respaldar las declaraciones de autoliquidación con desglose automatizado de ganancias y gastos deducibles de impuestos. Incorporar Assisted QuickBooks Online Accountant es gratis para los profesionales que ejercen trabajos contables, se hace por Zoom y está enfocada en los nuevos clientes que no están familiarizados con QuickBooks, pero también en aquellos clientes que no usan QuickBooks de forma activa. Es básicamente una clase para ir paso a paso con un tutor. Para celebrar el lanzamiento de la edición mundial de QBOA, se están ofreciendo precios fijos por los paquetes por tiempo limitado. Solo durante los próximos tres meses, la oferta de la suscripción al precio de un dólar seguirá estando activa para poder ayudar a los profesionales de la contabilidad a que sus clientes de las pequeñas empresas puedan acceder a QuickBooks online. La oferta implica pagar un dólar (o el precio equivalente según la moneda del país), por mes, por archivo. Está disponible para todos los países a fin de que todos puedan expandirse digitalmente. Los desarrolladores locales ahora pueden publicar aplicaciones como parte del QuickBooks Global App Store (para incluir nóminas, pagos, inventarios y aplicaciones creadas específicamente para ciertas regiones) e integrarlas al QuickBooks. Si desea obtener más información o quiere acceder gratis a la plataforma QuickBooks Online Accountant, por favor haga clic aquí. - FIN - Acerca de Intuit Intuit es una plataforma mundial del sector tecnofinanciero que busca impulsar a las personas y a las comunidades que la usan para que puedan prosperar. Cuenta con más de 100 millones de clientes en todo el mundo que usan TurboTax, Credit Karma, QuickBooks, y Mailchimp. Creemos que todos deberían tener la posibilidad de progresar. Por eso, siempre estamos buscando formas nuevas e innovadoras de hacer que esa posibilidad se cumpla. Visite nuestro sitio web para recibir más información sobre Intuit, sobre nuestros productos y servicios. Redes sociales. 1 World Economic Forum White Paper “Future Readiness of SMEs: Mobilizing the SME Sector to Drive Widespread Sustainability and Prosperity” publicado el 30 de noviembre de 2021: https://www.weforum.org/whitepapers/future-readiness-of-smes-mobilizing-the-sme-sector-to-drive-widespread-sustainability-and-prosperity 2 The US Bureau of Labor Statistics, 1994-2022: https://www.bls.gov/bdm/us_age_naics_00_table7.txt 3 ACCA (the Association of Chartered Certified Accountants) “Future ready: accountancy careers in the 2020s” report. Link: https://www.accaglobal.com/content/dam/ACCA_Global/professional-insights/FutureReady2020s/JamieLyon.FutureCareersAccoutancy2020s.summary.pdf El texto original en el idioma fuente de este comunicado es la versión oficial autorizada. title: Intuit QuickBooks lanza QuickBooks Online Accountant en más de 170 países de todo el mundo, incluido Costa Rica
text: SAN JOSÉ, Costa Rica--(BUSINESS WIRE)--Intuit QuickBooks anuncia hoy el acceso mundial gratuito a QuickBooks Online Accountant (QBOA), que permite a los profesionales de la contabilidad de 170 países pasarse a la nube más rápidamente, ampliar sus prácticas y aportar más valor a sus clientes y a la economía en general. Los profesionales de la contabilidad contribuyen enormemente a la economía a nivel local, regional y mundial, trabajando como socios comerciales de confianza de la comunidad de PYME, que representa el 90 % de las empresas y supone el 70 % del empleo mundial.1 Pero su impacto podría ser aún mayor, ya que la investigación de QuickBooks sugiere que los contadores están ahora más ocupados que nunca. Cuatro de cada 10 (43 %) afirman que tienen más de 40 clientes y el 86 % espera que la tecnología les ayude a ser más eficientes y a satisfacer mejor las necesidades de los clientes QBOA aborda esta cuestión permitiendo a los contadores gestionar el flujo de trabajo de su práctica, las interacciones con los clientes y la facturación, todo en un solo lugar. Desarrollado con un grupo selecto de contadores a nivel mundial durante los últimos 12 meses, QBOA es un conjunto refinado de beneficios destinado a ayudar a los contadores profesionales a trasladarse a la nube más rápido e impulsar una mayor eficiencia y escalabilidad de sus empresas. El acceso a información en tiempo real desde cualquier lugar del mundo sitúa a los contadores profesionales en el centro de la toma de decisiones empresariales informadas que podrían aumentar positivamente los índices de supervivencia de las pequeñas empresas (actualmente, solo el 51,6 % de las pequeñas empresas estadounidenses sobreviven más allá de los primeros cinco años).2 Intuit cuenta con más de 100 millones de clientes en todo el mundo y es una de las mayores empresas de software del mundo. Intuit QuickBooks es ahora el primer proveedor del mercado que ofrece servicios a esta escala para los profesionales de la contabilidad. Lars Leber, vicepresidente de Intuit QuickBooks Australia, afirmó: “Costa Rica es un país comprometido con el crecimiento y la modernización de sus infraestructuras. En los últimos años se ha actuado de forma generalizada para mejorar la fiabilidad del suministro eléctrico, un recurso esencial para el bienestar, pero también para crear oportunidades de trabajo. Con solo un acceso Wi-Fi, la plataforma QBOA puede utilizarse desde cualquier lugar del mundo, lo que aporta nuevos niveles de oportunidad a las comunidades más aisladas de las grandes ciudades, tanto para los contadores como para las pequeñas empresas a las que apoyan”. Las tendencias mundiales y los avances tecnológicos están transformando el mundo laboral y remodelando el futuro de la profesión contable, desplazando cada vez más la demanda hacia funciones más estratégicas y analíticas.3 Los contadores serán fundamentales para impulsar las empresas locales y la prosperidad económica. QuickBooks Online Accountant beneficia a los contadores profesionales de cualquier parte del mundo gracias al siguiente conjunto de funciones: ● Inicio de sesión único y vista en panel de los datos históricos y en tiempo real de los clientes, los proyectos en curso y los plazos; ● Transacciones automatizadas y reglas específicas que clasifican los datos, minimizando las entradas manuales de datos y reduciendo el riesgo de errores; ● Opciones para utilizar flujos de trabajo con el fin de enviar recordatorios a las personas adecuadas en el momento oportuno y automatizar tareas comunes, algo muy valioso para los contadores en los periodos más ajetreados del año; ● Aplicaciones integradas que ofrecen funcionalidades que abarcan la elaboración de informes, la gestión de inventarios, la gestión de gastos, las nóminas y el comercio electrónico; ● Capacidad predictiva basada en IA para estimar las contribuciones al impuesto sobre la renta/IVA, respaldar las declaraciones de autoliquidación con desglose automatizado de ganancias y gastos deducibles de impuestos. La integración asistida de QuickBooks Online Accountant para profesionales contables y tenedores de libros es libre, a través de una sesión de Zoom, y se centra en los nuevos clientes recién llegados a QuickBooks, así como cualquier cliente existente que no esté utilizando activamente QuickBooks. Es una sesión práctica, “Do-It-With-Me”. Para celebrar el lanzamiento de la edición global de QBOA, también se anunciaron paquetes de precios por tiempo limitado. Para ayudar a los contadores a que sus clientes de pequeñas empresas se inicien en QuickBooks online, sólo durante los próximos tres meses estará activa la oferta de suscripción de un dólar. Un dólar (o el equivalente en la moneda del país), al mes, por archivo. La oferta está disponible en todos los países, para apoyar aún más la expansión digital de todos los contadores. Los desarrolladores locales ya pueden publicar aplicaciones como parte del QuickBooks Global App Store (Tienda de aplicaciones QuickBooks Global) –para incluir nóminas, pagos, inventario y aplicaciones creadas específicamente para regiones concretas– para integrarse con QuickBooks. Para obtener más información y saber cómo acceder a la plataforma gratuita QuickBooks Online Accountant, ingrese aquí. - FIN - Acerca de Intuit Intuit es la plataforma global de tecnología financiera que impulsa la prosperidad de las personas y las comunidades a las que servimos. Con más de 100 millones de clientes en todo el mundo que utilizan TurboTax, Credit Karma, QuickBooks, y Mailchimp, creemos que todo el mundo debe tener la oportunidad de prosperar. Nunca dejamos de trabajar para encontrar formas nuevas e innovadoras de hacerlo posible. Visítenos para obtener la información más reciente acerca de Intuit, nuestros productos y servicios, y encuéntrenos en las redes sociales. 1Libro Blanco del Foro Económico Mundial “Future Readiness of SMEs: Mobilizing the SME Sector to Drive Widespread Sustainability and Prosperity” (Preparación de las PYME para el futuro: movilizar al sector de las PYME para impulsar la sostenibilidad y la prosperidad generalizadas) publicado el 30 de noviembre de 2021: https://www.weforum.org/whitepapers/future-readiness-of-smes-mobilizing-the-sme-sector-to-drive-widespread-sustainability-and-prosperity 2 Oficina de Estadísticas Laborales de EE. UU., 1994-2022: https://www.bls.gov/bdm/us_age_naics_00_table7.txt 3 Informe "Future ready: accountancy careers in the 2020s" (Preparados para el futuro: carreras contables en 2020) de ACCA (Asociación de Expertos Contables Colegiados). Enlace:https://www.accaglobal.com/content/dam/ACCA_Global/professional-insights/FutureReady2020s/JamieLyon.FutureCareersAccoutancy2020s.summary.pdf El texto original en el idioma fuente de este comunicado es la versión oficial autorizada. title: Intuit QuickBooks Launches QuickBooks Online Accountant in More Than 170 Countries Around the World, Including the Dominican Republic
text: SANTO DOMINGO, Dominican Republic--(BUSINESS WIRE)--Intuit QuickBooks today announces global free access to QuickBooks Online Accountant (QBOA), enabling accounting professionals in 170 countries to move to the cloud faster, scale their practices and bring more value to their clients and the wider economy. Accounting professionals make a huge contribution to the economy at a local, regional and global level, working as trusted business partners to the SMB community, which represents 90% of businesses and accounts for 70% of employment globally1. But their impact could be even bigger, as QuickBooks research suggests accountants are now busier than ever. Four out of 10 (43%) report they have more than 40 clients – and 86% are looking to technology to help them be more efficient and better meet client needs. QBOA addresses this by enabling accountants to manage their practice workflow, client interactions and billing all in one place. Access to real time insights from anywhere in the world puts professional accountants at the heart of driving informed business decisions that could positively increase small business survival rates – currently, only 51.6% of U.S. small businesses survive beyond the first five years2. Lars Leber, Vice President of Intuit QuickBooks Australia, said: “Efficient and effective financial management can be one of the greatest obstacles to a successful business. While policies have been launched in the Dominican Republic to make starting a business easier, the challenges remain with executing a contract taking more than four years. Access to QBOA for free means access to informed, structured guidance and essential resources for all those responsible for financial management and bookkeeping. By providing the platform for successful accounting, QuickBooks is providing the tools for widespread business success – not just in the Dominican Republic, but around the world.” Global trends and technological advancements are transforming the world of work and reshaping the future of the accountancy profession, increasingly shifting demand towards more strategic and analytical roles3. One dollar (or country currency equivalent), per month, per file. The deal is available in all countries, to further support digital expansion for all accountants. Local developers can now publish apps as part of the QuickBooks Global App Store – to include payroll, payments, inventory and purpose-built applications for specific regions – to integrate with QuickBooks. title: Intuit QuickBooks anuncia el lanzamiento de QuickBooks Online Accountant en más de 170 países, incluso en República Dominicana
text: SANTO DOMINGO, República Dominicana--(BUSINESS WIRE)--Intuit QuickBooks anuncia hoy el acceso gratuito a QuickBooks Online Accountant (QBOA), que les permite a los contadores de 170 países trabajar en la nube de manera más rápida, mejorar sus servicios y ofrecerle un valor agregado no solo a sus clientes sino también a la economía en general. Los contadores profesionales hacen un gran aporte a la economía local, regional y mundial, ya que son socios de confianza de todas las PYME, que representan al 90% de las empresas en total y que generan el 70% del empleo1. Sin embargo, su impacto podría ser aún mayor, tal como sugiere la investigación de QuickBooks los contadores tienen más trabajo que nunca. Cuatro de cada diez contadores (un 43%) informó tener más de 40 clientes y un 86% está en la búsqueda de tecnologías que los ayuden a ser más eficientes y a poder resolver las necesidades de sus clientes de una manera mejor. QBOA aborda esta cuestión porque permite que los contadores puedan manejar su flujo de trabajo, las interacciones con los clientes y la facturación, todo en un solo lugar. Gracias al desarrollo de un grupo selecto de contadores de todo el mundo que trabajaron durante los últimos 12 meses, QBOA logró convertirse en un paquete de trabajo mejorado con beneficios para que los contadores puedan pasar a la nube de manera rápida y tener una mayor eficiencia y escalabilidad en sus empresas. El poder acceder a la información en tiempo real desde cualquier lugar del mundo pone a los contadores en el centro de la toma de decisiones empresariales, las cuales podrían aumentar de manera positiva los índices de supervivencia de las pequeñas empresas que, actualmente, indican que solo un 51,6% de las pequeñas empresas de Estados Unidos se pueden sostener más allá de los cinco primeros años.2. Intuit ayuda a más de 100 millones de clientes de todo el mundo y es una de las empresas de software más grandes que existen. Ahora, se le suma Intuit Quickbook el primer proveedor del mercado que ofrece este tipo de servicios a esta escala para la profesión contable. Lars Leber, Vicepresidente de Intuit QuickBooks Australia, declaró: “Uno de los mayores obstáculos que existe para conseguir que una empresa tenga éxito es tener una gestión financiera que sea eficiente y eficaz. Si bien es cierto que en República Dominicana se han creado políticas que faciliten la creación de una empresa, todavía hay dificultades para llevar adelante aquellos contratos que duran más de cuatro años. Que QBOA sea de acceso gratuito implica un acceso gratuito a tener orientación informada y estructura, y recursos fundamentales para todos aquellos que son responsables de la gestión financiera y la contabilidad. Ya que ofrece una plataforma para poder ejercer la contabilidad con éxito, QuickBook proporciona también las herramientas necesarias para tener éxito empresarial. No solo en República Dominicana, sino en todos lados”. Las tendencias mundiales y los avances tecnológicos están cambiando nuestra manera de trabajar y están remodelando el futuro del trabajo contable, que hace que haya una mayor demanda de roles que sean más estratégicos y analíticos.3. Los contadores serán un eje central en el crecimiento de las empresas locales y en la prosperidad de la economía. QuickBooks Online Accountant es un beneficio para todos los contadores gracias a que proporciona las siguientes funciones: Inicio de sesión único, visualización del panel de control con datos de los clientes ya sean históricos o en tiempo real, proyectos actuales y fechas de entrega Transacciones automatizadas y reglas específicas para clasificar datos, que minimiza las entradas manuales de datos y reduce el riesgo de cometer errores Posibilidad de usar flujos de trabajo para enviar recordatorios a destinatarios específicos en un momento determinado y automatizar tareas básicas, muy valioso para los momentos del año en los que hay mucho trabajo Aplicaciones integradas con funciones para hacer informes, gestionar inventarios, gastos, nóminas y comercios virtuales (eCommerce) Capacidad predictiva basada en IA para estimar las contribuciones al impuesto sobre la renta/IVA, respaldar las declaraciones de autoliquidación con desglose automatizado de ganancias y gastos deducibles de impuestos Incorporar Assisted QuickBooks Online Accountant es gratis para los profesionales que ejercen trabajos contables, se hace por Zoom y está enfocada en los nuevos clientes que no están familiarizados con QuickBooks, pero también en aquellos clientes que no usan QuickBooks de forma activa. Es básicamente una clase para ir paso a paso con un tutor. Para celebrar el lanzamiento de la edición mundial de QBOA, se están ofreciendo precios fijos por los paquetes por tiempo limitado. Solo durante los próximos tres meses, la oferta de la suscripción al precio de un dólar seguirá estando activa para poder ayudar a los profesionales de la contabilidad a que sus clientes de las pequeñas empresas puedan acceder a QuickBooks online. La oferta implica pagar un dólar (o el precio equivalente según la moneda del país), por mes, por archivo. Está disponible para todos los países a fin de que todos puedan expandirse digitalmente. Los desarrolladores locales ahora pueden publicar aplicaciones como parte del QuickBooks Global App Store (para incluir nóminas, pagos, inventarios y aplicaciones creadas específicamente para ciertas regiones) e integrarlas al QuickBooks. Si desea obtener más información o quiere acceder gratis a la plataforma QuickBooks Online Accountant, por favor haga clic aquí. - FIN - Acerca de Intuit Intuit es una plataforma mundial del sector tecnofinanciero que busca impulsar a las personas y a las comunidades que la usan para que puedan prosperar. Cuenta con más de 100 millones de clientes en todo el mundo que usan TurboTax, Credit Karma, QuickBooks, y Mailchimp. Creemos que todos deberían tener la posibilidad de progresar. Por eso, siempre estamos buscando formas nuevas e innovadoras de hacer que esa posibilidad se cumpla. Visite nuestro sitio web para recibir más información sobre Intuit, sobre nuestros productos y servicios. También nos puede encontrar en nuestras redes sociales. 1 World Economic Forum White Paper “Future Readiness of SMEs: Mobilizing the SME Sector to Drive Widespread Sustainability and Prosperity” publicado el 30 de noviembre de 2021: https://www.weforum.org/whitepapers/future-readiness-of-smes-mobilizing-the-sme-sector-to-drive-widespread-sustainability-and-prosperity 2 The US Bureau of Labor Statistics, 1994-2022: https://www.bls.gov/bdm/us_age_naics_00_table7.txt 3 ACCA (Association of Chartered Certified Accountants) “Future ready: accountancy careers in the 2020s” report. Link: https://www.accaglobal.com/content/dam/ACCA_Global/professional-insights/FutureReady2020s/JamieLyon.FutureCareersAccoutancy2020s.summary.pdf El texto original en el idioma fuente de este comunicado es la versión oficial autorizada. Las traducciones solo se suministran como adaptación y deben cotejarse con el texto en el idioma fuente, que es la única versión del texto que tendrá un efecto legal.

title: Intuit QuickBooks Launches QuickBooks Online Accountant in More Than 170 Countries Around the World, Including South Africa
text: CAPE TOWN, South Africa--(BUSINESS WIRE)--Intuit QuickBooks today announces global free access to QuickBooks Online Accountant (QBOA), enabling accounting professionals in 170 countries to move to the cloud faster, scale their practices and bring more value to their clients and the wider economy. Accounting professionals make a huge contribution to the economy at a local, regional and global level, working as trusted business partners to the SMB community, which represents 90% of businesses and accounts for 70% of employment globally1. But their impact could be even bigger, as QuickBooks research suggests accountants are now busier than ever. Four out of 10 (43%) report they have more than 40 clients – and 86% are looking to technology to help them be more efficient and better meet client needs. QBOA addresses this by enabling accountants to manage their practice workflow, client interactions and billing all in one place. Developed with a select group of accountants globally over the past 12 months, QBOA is a refined suite of benefits to help professional accountants move to the cloud faster and drive greater efficiency and scalability of their firms. Access to real time insights from anywhere in the world puts professional accountants at the heart of driving informed business decisions that could positively increase small business survival rates – currently, only 51.6% of U.S. small businesses survive beyond the first five years2. Intuit serves more than 100 million customers worldwide and is one of the biggest software companies in the world, with Intuit QuickBooks now the first provider in the market to deliver at this scale for the accounting profession. Lars Leber, Vice President of Intuit QuickBooks Australia, said: “The global roll out of the QuickBooks Online Accountant platform offers a level of opportunity for growth and support, to both accountants and the small businesses they support, that has not been seen before. Here in South Africa, the QBOA platform has already proven to be an invaluable resource. With financial management processes a key area in which the government seeks to continually improve, the QBOA platform offers a complimentary resource to continue to grow and develop the country’s accounting capabilities.” Global trends and technological advancements are transforming the world of work and reshaping the future of the accountancy profession, increasingly shifting demand towards more strategic and analytical roles3. Accountants will be key to helping fuel local business and economic prosperity. Nadine Augustine, Eccom Accountants, South Africa, added: “QuickBooks Online Accountant has had a massive impact on my accounting practice in South Africa. I can now access all my clients’ files in one place and allocate work to the team driving greater efficiency. Being so easy to use, I have saved hours in admin each week, allowing me to put that time back into my clients and growing my practice.” QuickBooks Online Accountant benefits professional accountants anywhere in the world – through the following set of features: Singular login and dashboard view of real-time and historic client data, current projects and deadlines; Automated transactions and specific rules classifying data, minimizing manual data entries and reducing risk of errors; Options to use workflows to send reminders to the right people at the right time and automate common tasks –invaluable for bookkeepers in busiest periods of the year; Integrated apps providing functionality that spans reporting, inventory management, expense management, payroll, eCommerce; Predictive AI-based ability to estimate income tax/VAT contributions, support self-assessment filings with automated breakdown of earnings and tax-deductible expense. Assisted QuickBooks Online Accountant onboarding for accountant professionals and bookkeepers is free, via a Zoom session, and is focused on new clients who are new to QuickBooks, as well as any existing clients that aren’t actively using QuickBooks. It’s a hands-on, “Do-It-With-Me” session. To celebrate the launch of the QBOA global edition, limited time only pricing bundles have also been announced. To help accountants get their small business clients started on QuickBooks online, for just the next three months, the one-dollar subscription deal will be active. One dollar (or country currency equivalent), per month, per file. The deal is available in all countries, to further support digital expansion for all accountants. Local developers can now publish apps as part of the QuickBooks Global App Store – to include payroll, payments, inventory and purpose-built applications for specific regions – to integrate with QuickBooks. For more information and information on how to access the free QuickBooks Online Accountant platform please visit here. - ENDS - About Intuit Intuit is the global financial technology platform that powers prosperity for the people and communities we serve. With more than 100 million customers worldwide using TurboTax, Credit Karma, QuickBooks, and Mailchimp, we believe that everyone should have the opportunity to prosper. We never stop working to find new, innovative ways to make that possible. Please visit us for the latest information about Intuit, our products and services, and find us on social. 1 World Economic Forum White Paper “Future Readiness of SMEs: Mobilizing the SME Sector to Drive Widespread Sustainability and Prosperity” published 30 November 2021: https://www.weforum.org/whitepapers/future-readiness-of-smes-mobilizing-the-sme-sector-to-drive-widespread-sustainability-and-prosperity 2 The US Bureau of Labor Statistics, 1994-2022: https://www.bls.gov/bdm/us_age_naics_00_table7.txt 3 ACCA (the Association of Chartered Certified Accountants) “Future ready: accountancy careers in the 2020s” report. Link: https://www.accaglobal.com/content/dam/ACCA_Global/professional-insights/FutureReady2020s/JamieLyon.FutureCareersAccoutancy2020s.summary.pdf

title: Intuit QuickBooks Launches QuickBooks Online Accountant in More Than 170 Countries Around the World, Including United Arab Emirates
text: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates--(BUSINESS WIRE)--Intuit QuickBooks today announces global free access to QuickBooks Online Accountant (QBOA), enabling accounting professionals in 170 countries to move to the cloud faster, scale their practices and bring more value to their clients and the wider economy. Accounting professionals make a huge contribution to the economy at a local, regional and global level, working as trusted business partners to the SMB community, which represents 90% of businesses and accounts for 70% of employment globally1. But their impact could be even bigger, as QuickBooks research suggests accountants are now busier than ever. Four out of 10 (43%) report they have more than 40 clients – and 86% are looking to technology to help them be more efficient and better meet client needs. QBOA addresses this by enabling accountants to manage their practice workflow, client interactions and billing all in one place. Developed with a select group of accountants globally over the past 12 months, QBOA is a refined suite of benefits to help professional accountants move to the cloud faster and drive greater efficiency and scalability of their firms. Access to real time insights from anywhere in the world puts professional accountants at the heart of driving informed business decisions that could positively increase small business survival rates – currently, only 51.6% of U.S. small businesses survive beyond the first five years2. Intuit serves more than 100 million customers worldwide and is one of the biggest software companies in the world, with Intuit QuickBooks now the first provider in the market to deliver at this scale for the accounting profession. Lars Leber, Vice President of Intuit QuickBooks Australia, said: “Small businesses form a vital component of economic growth in the UAE3. Backed by government support and new legislation like the upcoming Small Business Relief Law, the number of small businesses in the region is set to boom - with the need for skilled accountants growing just as rapidly. Providing accountants with the ability to efficiently manage customers from one easy-to-use platform will therefore have a huge impact in the region. With just a Wi-Fi connection required, the global roll out of QBOA will create a positive effect for accounts and the SME owners they support, not only in the UAE, but around the world.” Global trends and technological advancements are transforming the world of work and reshaping the future of the accountancy profession, increasingly shifting demand towards more strategic and analytical roles4. The UAE has one of the most liberal trade regimes in the Gulf region and attracts strong capital flows from across the region5. Additionally, the UAE’s Ministry of Finance has raised the minimum threshold for corporate tax from $100,000 (AED375,000) to $816,000 (AED3 million) for small and micro businesses, startups and freelancers, while Small Business Relief Law is intended to support startups and other small or micro businesses by reducing their corporate tax burden, and where an accounting professional can provide support. The law will go into effect at the beginning of June 2023, and continue until the end of December 20266. Accountants will be key to helping fuel local business and economic prosperity. QuickBooks Online Accountant benefits professional accountants anywhere in the world – through the following set of features: Singular login and dashboard view of real-time and historic client data, current projects and deadlines; Automated transactions and specific rules classifying data, minimizing manual data entries and reducing risk of errors; Options to use workflows to send reminders to the right people at the right time and automate common tasks –invaluable for bookkeepers in busiest periods of the year; Integrated apps providing functionality that spans reporting, inventory management, expense management, payroll, eCommerce; Predictive AI-based ability to estimate income tax/VAT contributions, support self-assessment filings with automated breakdown of earnings and tax-deductible expense. Assisted QuickBooks Online Accountant onboarding for accountant professionals and bookkeepers is free, via a Zoom session, and is focused on new clients who are new to QuickBooks, as well as any existing clients that aren’t actively using QuickBooks. It’s a hands-on, “Do-It-With-Me” session. To celebrate the launch of the QBOA global edition, limited time only pricing bundles have also been announced. To help accountants get their small business clients started on QuickBooks online, for just the next three months, the one-dollar subscription deal will be active. One dollar (or country currency equivalent), per month, per file. The deal is available in all countries, to further support digital expansion for all accountants. Local developers can now publish apps as part of the QuickBooks Global App Store – to include payroll, payments, inventory and purpose-built applications for specific regions – to integrate with QuickBooks. For more information and information on how to access the free QuickBooks Online Accountant platform please visit here. - ENDS - About Intuit Intuit is the global financial technology platform that powers prosperity for the people and communities we serve. With more than 100 million customers worldwide using TurboTax, Credit Karma, QuickBooks, and Mailchimp, we believe that everyone should have the opportunity to prosper. We never stop working to find new, innovative ways to make that possible. Please visit us for the latest information about Intuit, our products and services, and find us on social. 1 World Economic Forum White Paper “Future Readiness of SMEs: Mobilizing the SME Sector to Drive Widespread Sustainability and Prosperity” published 30 November 2021: https://www.weforum.org/whitepapers/future-readiness-of-smes-mobilizing-the-sme-sector-to-drive-widespread-sustainability-and-prosperity 2 The US Bureau of Labor Statistics, 1994-2022: https://www.bls.gov/bdm/us_age_naics_00_table7.txt 3 PWC, ‘Doing Business in the UAE’ report: www.pwc.de/de/internationale-maerkte/assets/doing-business-in-the-uae.pdf 4 ACCA (the Association of Chartered Certified Accountants) “Future ready: accountancy careers in the 2020s” report. Link: https://www.accaglobal.com/content/dam/ACCA_Global/professional-insights/FutureReady2020s/JamieLyon.FutureCareersAccoutancy2020s.summary.pdf 5 PWC, ‘Doing Business in the UAE’ report: www.pwc.de/de/internationale-maerkte/assets/doing-business-in-the-uae.pdf 6 Wamda,com, April 2023: https://www.wamda.com/2023/04/uae-raises-tax-threshold-startups#:~:text=The%20UAE's%20Ministry%20of%20Finance,micro%20businesses%2C%20startups%20and%20freelancers.

title: Intuit QuickBooks Launches QuickBooks Online Accountant in More Than 170 Countries Around the World, Including Malaysia
text: KUALA LUMPUR, Malaysia--(BUSINESS WIRE)--Intuit QuickBooks today announces global free access to QuickBooks Online Accountant (QBOA), enabling accounting professionals in 170 countries to move to the cloud faster, scale their practices and bring more value to their clients and the wider economy. Accounting professionals make a huge contribution to the economy at a local, regional and global level, working as trusted business partners to the SMB community, which represents 90% of businesses and accounts for 70% of employment globally1. But their impact could be even bigger, as QuickBooks research suggests accountants are now busier than ever. Four out of 10 (43%) report they have more than 40 clients – and 86% are looking to technology to help them be more efficient and better meet client needs. QBOA addresses this by enabling accountants to manage their practice workflow, client interactions and billing all in one place. Developed with a select group of accountants globally over the past 12 months, QBOA is a refined suite of benefits to help professional accountants move to the cloud faster and drive greater efficiency and scalability of their firms. Access to real time insights from anywhere in the world puts professional accountants at the heart of driving informed business decisions that could positively increase small business survival rates – currently, only 51.6% of U.S. small businesses survive beyond the first five years2. Intuit serves more than 100 million customers worldwide and is one of the biggest software companies in the world, with Intuit QuickBooks now the first provider in the market to deliver at this scale for the accounting profession. Lars Leber, Vice President of Intuit QuickBooks Australia, said: “As a cost-competitive market ranking in the top 20 of the world’s most business-friendly countries,3 Malaysia is an attractive market for business owners – creating a wealth of opportunities for the region’s accounting professionals. The scale of the roll out of QBOA, and the accessibility of the platform for free, will help to unlock huge economic potential in Malaysia, as well as around the globe.” Global trends and technological advancements are transforming the world of work and reshaping the future of the accountancy profession, increasingly shifting demand towards more strategic and analytical roles4. Malaysia is in need of 60,000 qualified professional accountants by 2030, with just over 37,000 members registered with the Malaysian Institute of Accountants (MIA) today.5 Accountants will be key to helping fuel local business and economic prosperity. Chun How Cheah, HighPines, Malaysia, added: “Using QuickBooks Online Accountant in Singapore has opened us up to the future of accounting and global commerce. It has seen us increase efficiency and profitability of our firm by helping us manage our work and clients in one, easy-to-use platform. It’s the must-have accounting software for those looking to grow and scale up faster. We are excited that the release of Global QBOA will unlock efficiencies in new countries and regions for our firm.” QuickBooks Online Accountant benefits professional accountants anywhere in the world – through the following set of features: Singular login and dashboard view of real-time and historic client data, current projects and deadlines; Automated transactions and specific rules classifying data, minimizing manual data entries and reducing risk of errors; Options to use workflows to send reminders to the right people at the right time and automate common tasks – invaluable for bookkeepers in busiest periods of the year; Integrated apps providing functionality that spans reporting, inventory management, expense management, payroll, eCommerce; Predictive AI-based ability to estimate income tax/VAT contributions, support self-assessment filings with automated breakdown of earnings and tax-deductible expense. Assisted QuickBooks Online Accountant onboarding for accountant professionals and bookkeepers is free, via a Zoom session, and is focused on new clients who are new to QuickBooks, as well as any existing clients that aren’t actively using QuickBooks. It’s a hands-on, “Do-It-With-Me” session. To celebrate the launch of the QBOA global edition, limited time only pricing bundles have also been announced. To help accountants get their small business clients started on QuickBooks online, for just the next three months, the one-dollar subscription deal will be active. One dollar (or country currency equivalent), per month, per file. The deal is available in all countries, to further support digital expansion for all accountants. Local developers can now publish apps as part of the QuickBooks Global App Store – to include payroll, payments, inventory and purpose-built applications for specific regions – to integrate with QuickBooks. For more information and information on how to access the free QuickBooks Online Accountant platform please visit here. - ENDS - About Intuit Intuit is the global financial technology platform that powers prosperity for the people and communities we serve. With more than 100 million customers worldwide using TurboTax, Credit Karma, QuickBooks, and Mailchimp, we believe that everyone should have the opportunity to prosper. We never stop working to find new, innovative ways to make that possible. title: Intuit QuickBooks Launches QuickBooks Online Accountant in More Than 170 Countries Around the World, Including Hong Kong
text: HONG KONG--(BUSINESS WIRE)--Intuit QuickBooks today announces global free access to QuickBooks Online Accountant (QBOA), enabling accounting professionals in 170 countries to move to the cloud faster, scale their practices and bring more value to their clients and the wider economy. Accounting professionals make a huge contribution to the economy at a local, regional and global level, working as trusted business partners to the SMB community, which represents 90% of businesses and accounts for 70% of employment globally1. But their impact could be even bigger, as QuickBooks research suggests accountants are now busier than ever. Four out of 10 (43%) report they have more than 40 clients – and 86% are looking to technology to help them be more efficient and better meet client needs. QBOA addresses this by enabling accountants to manage their practice workflow, client interactions and billing all in one place. Developed with a select group of accountants globally over the past 12 months, QBOA is a refined suite of benefits to help professional accountants move to the cloud faster and drive greater efficiency and scalability of their firms. Access to real time insights from anywhere in the world puts professional accountants at the heart of driving informed business decisions that could positively increase small business survival rates – currently, only 51.6% of U.S. small businesses survive beyond the first five years2. Intuit serves more than 100 million customers worldwide and is one of the biggest software companies in the world, with Intuit QuickBooks now the first provider in the market to deliver at this scale for the accounting profession. Lars Leber, Vice President of Intuit QuickBooks Australia, said: “Hong Kong is regularly recognized as one of the best places globally for running a business, consistently placing in the top five ‘Ease of Doing Business’ rankings. Accountants in the region play a key role in helping these businesses to adapt and prosper. With the rollout of QBOA, every accountant and bookkeeper can efficiently manage their clients from one easy-to-manage platform. Any professional accountant with an internet connection will benefit from financial management innovation fit for the modern age, while businesses benefit from improved expert support.” Global trends and technological advancements are transforming the world of work and reshaping the future of the accountancy profession, increasingly shifting demand towards more strategic and analytical roles3. Hong Kong is one of the world’s most competitive business centers, with the digitization of over 360,000 SMEs, constituting more than 98% of business establishments and employing about 44% of the workforce in the private sector expected to provide further boost to the economy4. Accountants will be key to helping fuel local business and economic prosperity. A recent QuickBooks study suggested that 93% of UK small businesses believe their business is more likely to survive with an accountant on board. QuickBooks Online Accountant benefits professional accountants anywhere in the world – through the following set of features: Singular login and dashboard view of real-time and historic client data, current projects and deadlines; Automated transactions and specific rules classifying data, minimizing manual data entries and reducing risk of errors; Options to use workflows to send reminders to the right people at the right time and automate common tasks – invaluable for bookkeepers in busiest periods of the year; Integrated apps providing functionality that spans reporting, inventory management, expense management, payroll, eCommerce; Predictive AI-based ability to estimate income tax/VAT contributions, support self-assessment filings with automated breakdown of earnings and tax-deductible expense. It’s a hands-on, “Do-It-With-Me” session. To celebrate the launch of the QBOA global edition, limited time only pricing bundles have also been announced. To help accountants get their small business clients started on QuickBooks online, for just the next three months, the one-dollar subscription deal will be active. One dollar (or country currency equivalent), per month, per file. The deal is available in all countries, to further support digital expansion for all accountants. Local developers can now publish apps as part of the QuickBooks Global App Store – to include payroll, payments, inventory and purpose-built applications for specific regions – to integrate with QuickBooks. title: Textron Aviation Brings Peace-of-Mind Technology to the Beechcraft Denali Cockpit
text: WICHITA, Kan.--(BUSINESS WIRE)--Textron Aviation announces that it is bringing the revolutionary Garmin Emergency Autoland system to its Beechcraft Denali single-engine turboprop.
